title: Binary backup and atomic swap
action: Create src/update/swap.rs (declared via `mod swap;` in src/update/mod.rs) with `pub fn backup_and_swap_binary(target_path: &std::path::Path, new_bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<std::path::PathBuf, SwapError>`, parameterized by target_path specifically so tests never touch this test process's own real executable. It must: read target_path's current bytes and write them to a sibling backup file (name pattern: `<filename>.bak-<unix-nanos-timestamp>` next to target_path); write new_bytes to a temp file in target_path's own parent directory, then atomically rename the temp file over target_path (write-then-rename, never write target_path directly in place -- an interrupted direct write must never leave a truncated binary); on unix only (`#[cfg(unix)]`), explicitly set the swapped-in file's permissions to mode 0o755 via `std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt` (a plain file write does not preserve/set the executable bit). Return the backup file's path on success. Add a thin, deliberately-untested wrapper `pub fn backup_and_swap_running_binary(new_bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<std::path::PathBuf, SwapError>` that calls the pure function with `std::env::current_exe()`. Do not touch src/main.rs, src/config/*, or src/update/github.rs/checksum.rs/asset.rs in this cell.

truths: Calling backup_and_swap_binary on a temp file creates a backup containing the ORIGINAL content, and the target path afterward contains the NEW bytes
On unix, the swapped-in file has the executable permission bit set afterward

artifacts.substantive: exports backup_and_swap_binary, backup_and_swap_running_binary, SwapError with the 2 named unit tests (the permission test cfg(unix)-gated), no TODO stubs

prohibitions: No changes to src/main.rs, src/config/*, or the other src/update/*.rs files in this cell
Tests must use a temp file as target_path, never the real running test binary

verify: test -f src/update/swap.rs && grep -q 'mod swap' src/update/mod.rs && grep -q 'fn backup_and_swap_binary_backs_up_old_content_and_writes_new_bytes' src/update/swap.rs && grep -q 'fn backup_and_swap_binary_sets_executable_permission_on_unix' src/update/swap.rs && cargo test --quiet update

trace.outcome: Added src/update/swap.rs: pure backup_and_swap_binary(target_path, new_bytes) that reads+backs up current bytes to a sibling .bak-<nanos> file, writes new bytes to a temp file in the same dir, sets 0o755 on unix, then renames atomically over target_path; plus thin untested backup_and_swap_running_binary wrapper over std::env::current_exe(). Declared via 'mod swap;' in src/update/mod.rs.
trace.verify_output: 27 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored (includes the 2 new swap.rs unit tests)
trace.verify_command: test -f src/update/swap.rs && grep -q 'mod swap' src/update/mod.rs && grep -q 'fn backup_and_swap_binary_backs_up_old_content_and_writes_new_bytes' src/update/swap.rs && grep -q 'fn backup_and_swap_binary_sets_executable_permission_on_unix' src/update/swap.rs && cargo test --quiet update
